This market refers on the tennis match between Juncheng Shang and Roberto Bautista Agut in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 17 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Shang' if Juncheng Shang advances against Roberto Bautista Agut.

This market will resolve to 'Agut' if Roberto Bautista Agut advances against Juncheng Shang.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Resolution will be based on official Grand Slam match statistics.

This market refers to the tennis match between Rei Sakamoto and Rafael Jodar in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 18 2026.
This market will resolve to "Over" if the total number of games completed across all sets equals or exceeds 41. Otherwise, it will resolve to "Under."

This market refers on the tennis match between Ashlyn Krueger and Sara Bejlek in the Australian Open WTA, scheduled for January 17 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Krueger' if Ashlyn Krueger advances against Sara Bejlek.

This market will resolve to 'Bejlek' if Sara Bejlek advances against Ashlyn Krueger.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

This market refers on the tennis match between Juan Manuel Cerundolo and Jordan Thompson in the Australian Open ATP, scheduled for January 17 at 7:00PM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Cerundolo' if Juan Manuel Cerundolo advances against Jordan Thompson.

This market will resolve to 'Thompson' if Jordan Thompson advances against Juan Manuel Cerundolo.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the Australian Open. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

Any first-set tiebreak counts as one (1) game toward the total.
